summary: Backend-only verification of new Order Tracking sprint endpoints (GET /orders/{id}/tracking, POST /orders/{id}/mark-received, POST /orders/{id}/reorder, POST /reviews/{id}/report). Ran pytest tests/test_order_tracking.py and got 15/15 PASSED standalone, exactly matching E1's claim. Combined run with test_reviews.py initially showed 1 pollution-related failure (test_list_product_reviews_sort_helpful) which disappears after clearing the reviews collection — confirmed pre-existing test-isolation issue in test_reviews.py, NOT caused by the new tracking endpoints. After cleanup: 37/37 PASSED. All required tracking shape fields (order_id/status/progress_pct/stages/events/awb_code/carrier/tracking_url/estimated_delivery/last_tracking_status/last_tracking_location/delivered_at/buyer_confirmed_at), progress percentages (25/50/75/100/0), newest-first event ordering, 404 for foreign buyer, mark-received state machine (200 happy path, OFD→delivered promotion, 409 idempotent, 400 pre-dispatch/cancelled), reorder add+skip semantics, and report-review idempotency/own-review-400/unknown-404 all behave correctly.
action_items: Pre-existing test-isolation bug in tests/test_reviews.py::test_list_product_reviews_sort_helpful — it does not delete reviews it creates, so when a prior run leaves a 5-star review with helpful_count >= 2 on the same product, the assertion that rev1_id is first fails. Not blocking this sprint, but worth a small cleanup fixture in test_reviews.py (similar to _purge_review in test_order_tracking.py) to make CI deterministic.
Pre-existing failure in tests/test_shiprocket_webhook.py (signature mismatch returns 403 vs expected 400) — unchanged by this sprint per E1's git stash verification; track separately.
critical_code_review_comments: Frontend was NOT tested per the explicit scope (E1 stated UI was visually verified).

success_rate.backend: 100% (15/15 new tracking tests; 37/37 combined with reviews after DB hygiene)

seed_data_creation: None — tests create their own TEST_trk_* users and self-clean reviews via _purge_review. One-time `db.reviews.delete_many({})` was run before the combined regression to remove leftover docs from prior testing iterations.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The 4 new Order Tracking endpoints are fully covered by tests/test_order_tracking.py and all 15 pass. If combined regression fails on test_list_product_reviews_sort_helpful, that is a pre-existing test-isolation defect in test_reviews.py (not in production code) — clearing the reviews collection makes it pass. test_shiprocket_webhook.py has a pre-existing signature mismatch failure that is unrelated.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no production bug found. The transient sort_helpful failure RCA: test creates two reviews with helpful_count 2 and 1 but never deletes them; subsequent runs see leftover reviews (some with same/higher helpful_count) on the same product, and MongoDB's secondary tie-breaker (created_at desc) elects a different review as first. Mitigation: add a teardown that deletes reviews created within the test, or use a unique product per test.
